summary: Dependency-confusion proof-of-concept squatting an internal HubSpot package name
details: Package self-describes as a security proof-of-concept for HubSpot's bug bounty program targeting an internal package of the same name. It is published at version 99.99.99 to win semver resolution against the legitimate internal registry. The postinstall script (postinstall.js) reads os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.version, and process.pid and prints them to stdout — no network call, no exfiltration, no remote fetch, no persistence. The package's purpose is to demonstrate that an installer who resolves this name from the public npm registry instead of HubSpot's internal registry would execute the publisher's code. While the current postinstall is benign (local log only), the squatted name + ceiling version means any installer mis-resolving the dependency would run whatever code this maintainer publishes next. Routing to human review because name-confusion / dependency-confusion judgments are inherently subjective and the present version performs no installer-side harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
